title: Ivory Coast vs Ecuador team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN
original_text:
Ivory Coast vs Ecuador team news and predicted lineups | Squawka
Harry Edwards
14 hours ago
Ivory Coast and Ecuador get their 2026 World Cup campaigns underway when they face off on Monday at 12am (UK time).
Les Elephants are back at the World Cup after missing the past two tournaments. Although they have won a game at each of their three previous World Cup appearances, Ivory Coast are yet to make it past the group stages. They’ll feel positive about their chances this year, but a lot will rely on this result.
Ecuador, meanwhile, are at their second consecutive World Cup and fifth tournament overall. They reached the last-16 in 2016 but have bowed out at the group stage in each of their past two appearances. Like Ivory Coast, they’ll be battling for a second-placed finish in Group C. Or hoping for a strong standing as a third-placed side.
This will be the first ever meeting between these two nations.

Ivory Coast vs Ecuador team news
Ivory Coast team news
Emerse Fae had to make a change to his initial World Cup squad through injury, with Clement Akpa replaced by Christopher Operi. It’s a significant change, as Akpa had been expected to start at left-back this summer. Operi didn’t start Ivory Coast’s friendly against France in his place, though. Ghislain Konan was on the left and may continue there at the World Cup, with Guela Doue on the right.
Yahia Fofana is firmly Ivory Coast’s first-choice goalkeeper, starting ahead of Alban Lafont and Mohamed Kone. Wilfried Singo and Emmanuel Agbadou appear to have emerged as the starting centre-back pairing. But Odilon Koussounou will be hoping to make an appearance. The midfield setup has changed in recent times, with Fae option for a 4-4-2 against France — as opposed to the three-man system he had been using.
Franck Kessie and Seko Fofana were the central midfield pairing against France, with Oumar Diakite and Yan Diomande on the wings. The latter has been tipped for a breakout tournament, while the likes of Amad Diallo, Nicolas Pepe and Evann Guessand are ready to make an impact from the bench. Elye Wahi will be the main goalscoring outlet, but he has been partnered by Simon Adingra, playing in a more central attacking role.

Ecuador team news
Ecuador manager Sebastian Beccacece has built a squad that can be flexible in terms of formations. Given the strength Ivory Coast boast in attack, he may opt for a 3-4-3 that will allow them to be defensively solid but also good on the counter. But we’re likely to see some changes from their final friendly starting XI against Guatemala.
Piero Hincapie and Willian Pacho will form part of the back three, having both joined the squad late due to the Champions League final. Felix Torres is likely to join them, as the favoured centre-back across the year so far. With a back three, Alan Franco should be pushed into the right wing-back role. While Franco is a central midfielder for his club, he has been operating as a right-sided defender for Ecuador. Pervis Estupinan should start on the left.
Moises Caicedo is another guaranteed starter, having been rested during the pre-tournament friendlies. Anthony Valencia has been favourite this year, so is the most likely to partner Caicedo. Ecuador can be fluid in attack in terms of positions. But Enner Valencia is expected to be the main goalscoring option, joined by Kendry Paez and Gonzalo Plata.
Ivory Coast vs Ecuador predicted lineups
Ivory Coast (4-4-2): Y Fofana; Doue, Singo, Agbadou, Konan; Diakite, S Fofana, Kessie, Diomande; Adingra, Wahi
Ecuador (3-4-3): Galindez; Pacho, Torres, Hincapie; Franco, A Valencia, M Caicedo, Estupinan; Plata, Valencia, Paez.
